sourceMovie.title: The Girl on the Train
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Girl on the Train (2016) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bone Collector
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Girl on the Train clicked for you, The Bone Collector (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Girl on the Train?
faq.answer: Try The Bone Collector, The Number 23, A Walk Among the Tombstones—each shares DNA with The Girl on the Train while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Girl on the Train?
faq.answer: The Girl on the Train (2016) is best described as Crime, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Girl on the Train worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
